epic.arc: One human holds a growing autonomous system through a single leveraged stamp. Everything that needs bdo arrives briefed at the scale he steers — epics and their arcs, not tickets — value first, mechanism after, on a surface he can open anywhere. Everything he decides is one line the whole machine obeys, and the system spends his attention like it is the most expensive resource it has, because it is.
epic.context: The doctrine's pulley (§3): a little input from the owner holds a big autonomous load. D-4 makes bdo the last stop on everything, which means the human stage is the system's permanent bottleneck — so its interface quality is not a nicety, it is the throughput of the whole machine. bdo's amendments drive this epic: too-thin tickets (rcp.efd40f7169c8), then ticket-scale itself rejected in favour of epic-scale steering.
epic.value: Your stamp stays leverage as the system grows: you see arcs of value with their context, judge in minutes from wherever you are, and the queue refuses to flood you.
epic.horizon: A served, authenticated page you open from any device you happen to be on: the whole system's open questions told as stories under their epics, cleared with a single gesture, with epic-level verdicts available when an arc should be steered as one unit.

pieces.glue: the briefed surface: every open item told value-first with what each verdict does, rendered to a page readable on any device, cleared through one pen
this directive itself: items file under epics, the inbox briefs the arc before the item, and the owner steers at epic scale
the first external surface, wired both ways — parked by bdo's amend in favour of the pattern (rcp.558ad49545cd)
the distributed half of the harness: every surface bdo steers from is registered and watched, its words reflected onto the log, so the stamp travels at whatever speed he does

the hand that lets bdo step out of the merge seat: an independent arc-scale gate judging a finished epic 'safe to land on main?' against the setpoints and gate history, never signing its own line — the move done-line 0028 named and deferred (draining the PR queue, not just the atom queue). Its eyes already exist: loop/digest.py (done-line 0032), the read-only fold bdo watches instead of operating the merge. Until bdo admits the node real (--by bdo) and the 'bdo merges' hard rule is amended, it merges nothing and he still merges; both triggers are his alone (D-4)

the records door (done-line 0172): a records-only PR — a session report or done-line, no atom — is backed without ceremony, the sibling of the phrasing door (atom.phrasing-backdoor.v0). A §9 session report written after its work already landed cannot bundle retroactively, yet belongs on main as the durable record; this lets it reach main, so the owner's record-keeping is never stranded off-trunk

the issue pen gains open + reopen — a session puts an owner decision on bdo's GitHub surface as a recorded act (instead of raw gh or a CLI-at-owner line) and can reverse a wrong/stale close with the same accountability; the #348 recovery lesson, governing the owner surface.

the queue refuses to flood you, made mechanical: an accreting owner inbox (open-only owner-ask mirrors, process-bound gate-tracker closes) is made over into one synthesized digest line, proven-resolved noise auto-silenced under a fence bdo stamps, and the genuinely-unresolved escalated by name (#628). Serves the arc's 'the queue refuses to flood you' value — a read-only fold (loop/reconcile_noise.py) computes each noise subject's resolution from the log alone, and the actuator silences only what bdo's noise_silence_fence authorizes (cooling-only, default-inert).
